---
title: "Bioinformatics Pitfalls Lab"
subtitle: "Small mistakes, big consequences."
---

The Bioinformatics Pitfalls Lab is a collection of short practical modules about common mistakes that can quietly distort biological interpretation.

These are not textbook chapters. They are debugging notes from the messy middle of analysis, where files, assumptions and metadata have to line up before results can be trusted.

## Modules

| Module | Focus | Status |
|---|---|---|
| [Wrong Genome Build](wrong-genome-build.qmd) | Reference genome mismatch | Ready |
| [Batch Effects](batch-effects.qmd) | Technical structure mistaken for biology | Ready |

## How to read these modules

Each module follows the same structure:

1. The mistake  
2. Why it happens  
3. What breaks  
4. How to detect it  
5. How to prevent it  
6. Tiny demo, figure, table or code snippet  
7. Related project  
8. Related Crosswalk post  
9. Related Concept Atlas entries
